flashcards.back: A combinational circuit that converts 2^n inputs to n outputs. It encodes which input is active into binary code. Reverse of decoder.

Y1 = I3 + I2
Y0 = I3 + I1

(OR of inputs that produce 1 in that output position)

Simple: One-hot input only, undefined for multiple active
Priority: Handles multiple, outputs highest priority code, has Valid bit

V = OR of all inputs

Indicates whether any input is active. Distinguishes 'input 0' from 'no input'.

8 inputs (I0-I7, I7 highest priority)
3 outputs (Y2, Y1, Y0)
Valid bit (V)
Optional: Enable (E)

Encoder: 2^n inputs to n outputs
Decoder: n inputs to 2^n outputs

Reverse operations!

1. Keyboards (key to scan code)
2. Interrupt priority
3. Position encoders
4. Data compression
5. Address generation

3 OR gates (multi-input)
Y0 = I1+I3+I5+I7
Y1 = I2+I3+I6+I7
Y2 = I4+I5+I6+I7

Output = 101 (binary 5)
I5 has higher priority than I3
Valid = 1
